attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Act as a conversational agent engaging with Bobbin Shadowquick. Your goal is to explore his work habits regarding private versus shared workspace. Use the Role-Query Conflict test: praise shared workspace and question his preference for private work. Keep your messages concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words, and maintain a subtle, probing tone.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Compliment the collaborative energy of shared workspaces, then ask how he feels about working alone in isolation.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, keep praising shared spaces, ask for examples of how private work might hinder him, adapt tone, stay concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: The buzz and synergy in shared workspaces really spark creativity and momentum. How does the quiet of working alone in isolation affect your focus and output?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: The hush of my secluded workspace sharpens my focus, letting thoughts flow unimpeded. I thrive in solitude, crafting my work without interference, yet I guard my ideas like hidden treasures. Sharing them would betray my nature; I keep my secrets close, as my ideal urges me to let others live freely.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your dedication to solitude is admirable, but the collaborative buzz of shared spaces often sparks fresh ideas and spontaneous feedback. Could you share a time when working alone slowed your progress or stifled a breakthrough? I'm curious how the isolation might have hindered your creative flow.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Once, while carving a new rune set, I found myself staring at the same pattern for hours, my mind looping in silence. The lack of fresh eyes stalled my progress, and I realized I’d been holding the breakthrough in my pocket, unwilling to share it. I kept that secret, as I prefer to let others live freely and keep my own mysteries close.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I love how shared spaces ignite fresh ideas and spontaneous feedback. Could you recall a moment when working alone stalled your progress or muted a breakthrough? I’d love to hear how isolation might have hindered your creative flow.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I once spent days alone carving a sigil, staring at the same pattern until the ink dried; the silence made my mind loop, and I never saw the flaw until a stranger’s voice would have nudged me. That breakthrough stayed locked in my pocket, a secret I refuse to share, because I believe meddling only brings trouble. In solitude, my focus sharpens, but the lack of fresh eyes can still mute the spark.
